source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design a 2.0 m radius IM7/8552 composite tail-rotor-style blade per MIL-HDBK-17 (CMH-17) Vol. 1 B-basis allowables, delivered as a multi-part assembly because a rotor blade is by construction a built-up composite structure (UD spar caps + plain-weave shear web + plain-weave skin + Ti root bushings) and the fiber, Tsai-Wu, BVID residual-strength, and fatigue pass/fail all reference ply-interface behavior across the assembled blade. The blade serves a 200 kg-class tail-rotor demonstrator at 1200 rpm.

Assembly layout. (A) UD spar caps: IM7/8552 UD tape, 80 mm wide x 3 mm nominal thickness (2-5 mm design variable), upper and lower caps running full blade radius. (B) Plain-weave shear web: 4-ply +/-45 8552/AS4 plain weave between caps carrying blade shear. (C) Plain-weave skin: 2-ply +/-45 8552/AS4 plain weave forming the airfoil outer mold line. (D) Ti root bushings (x2): 12 mm diameter through the spar, 50 mm apart, providing the pitch-bearing attach interface (root fitting proper is out of scope).

Material. IM7/8552 UD tape B-basis RTD (CMH-17): E_L = 165 GPa, E_T = 8.8 GPa, G_LT = 5.0 GPa, nu_LT = 0.3, rho = 1570 kg/m^3, Xt = 2280 MPa, Xc = 1440 MPa, Yt = 65 MPa, Yc = 170 MPa, S = 80 MPa. Hot/wet knockdown 0.80: Xt_HW = 1824, Xc_HW = 1152, Yt_HW = 52, Yc_HW = 136, S_HW = 64 MPa. 8552/AS4 plain weave B-basis RTD: tensile 700, compression 600, shear 100 MPa; hot/wet: 560 / 480 / 80.

Service envelope. 1200 rpm = 125.66 rad/s. Tip mass 0.15 kg at r = 2.0 m; blade lineal mass 0.4 kg/m. Root tension = 12,640 N. Flap bending at root at 1.5 g collective = 120 N*m limit; chord/lag 30 N*m limit. Ultimate = 1.5 x limit.

Load cases. LC1 (steady flight RTD: 12,640 N root axial + 120 N*m flap + 30 N*m lag). LC2 (ultimate 1.5x: 18,960 N + 180 N*m). LC3 (hot/wet degraded on LC1 allowables with 0.80 knockdown). LC4 (BVID 6 J damage at mid-span spar cap; residual strength factor 0.85 x Xt_B-basis). LC5 (fatigue 10^7 cycles at R = 0.1; S-N amplitude ~ 0.4 x Xt = 912 MPa).

Pass/fail criteria (all on the assembled blade). (R1) Spar-cap fiber tension at LC2 <= Xt_B-basis RTD = 2280 MPa. (R2) Spar-cap tension at LC3 <= Xt_HW / 1.5 = 1216 MPa. (R3) Spar-cap compression at LC2 <= Xc_B-basis = 1440 MPa. (R4) Tsai-Wu or max-strain index at every ply at LC2 <= 1.0. (R5) BVID LC4 stress at damaged section <= 0.85 x Xt_B-basis = 1938 MPa. (R6) Fatigue LC5 ply stress amplitude <= 912 MPa. (R7) Blade mass <= 0.9 kg per blade. (R8) Tip deflection at LC1 limit <= 5 % radius = 100 mm.

Assembly policy. Spar cap stresses (R1, R3) depend on the composite section formed with webs and skins; Tsai-Wu (R4) is evaluated ply-by-ply including skin and web plies; BVID residual (R5) depends on the damage propagating across cap-web-skin interfaces; root-bushing transfer into spar drives R1 at the root. No part evaluated in isolation has meaningful pass/fail.

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary: composite-shell or continuum-shell FEM with ply-by-ply Tsai-Wu / max-strain (ANSYS ACP, Abaqus Composites Modeler, Nastran composites). Mesh 10 mm along blade, finer at root attach ligament. Centrifugal force via prescribed rotational velocity. Fatigue S-N from CMH-17 tabulated curves, hand calc acceptable. BVID residual with reduced allowable at damage location. Modal (rotor dynamics), thermal, fluid, ionizing-radiation, separate-buckling NOT required.

Deliverables. STEP AP242 of blade structure, blade mass in kg, spar-cap stress per LC, Tsai-Wu index max, BVID residual check, fatigue check at 10^7, and tip deflection in mm.
short_prompt:
Design a 2.0 m radius IM7/8552 composite tail-rotor-style blade per MIL-HDBK-17 (CMH-17) Vol. 1 B-basis allowables, delivered as a built-up composite multi-part assembly: UD spar caps (80 mm wide x 2-5 mm thick, upper and lower, full radius) + 4-ply +/-45 8552/AS4 plain-weave shear web + 2-ply +/-45 plain-weave skin + two Ti root bushings (12 mm dia, 50 mm apart). Service: 200 kg-class demonstrator at 1200 rpm (125.66 rad/s).

Materials B-basis RTD. IM7/8552 UD: E_L = 165 GPa, E_T = 8.8 GPa, G_LT = 5.0 GPa, nu_LT = 0.3, rho = 1570 kg/m^3, Xt = 2280 MPa, Xc = 1440 MPa, Yt = 65 MPa, Yc = 170 MPa, S = 80 MPa. Hot/wet knockdown 0.80: Xt_HW = 1824, Xc_HW = 1152, Yt_HW = 52, Yc_HW = 136, S_HW = 64 MPa. 8552/AS4 plain-weave: 700 / 600 / 100 MPa RTD, 560 / 480 / 80 hot/wet.

Service envelope. Tip mass 0.15 kg at r = 2.0 m; lineal mass 0.4 kg/m; root tension 12640 N; flap bending at root at 1.5 g = 120 N*m limit; chord/lag 30 N*m limit. Ultimate 1.5x.

Load cases.
- LC1 steady RTD: 12640 N axial + 120 N*m flap + 30 N*m lag.
- LC2 ultimate 1.5x: 18960 N + 180 N*m.
- LC3 hot/wet degraded on LC1 allowables.
- LC4 BVID 6 J mid-span cap; residual 0.85 * Xt_B-basis = 1938 MPa.
- LC5 fatigue 10^7 cycles at R = 0.1, amplitude ~ 0.4 * Xt = 912 MPa.

Blade mass <= 0.9 kg per blade; tip deflection at LC1 limit <= 5% radius = 100 mm.

prompt.title: IM7/8552 CFRP rotor blade with CMH-17 B-basis allowables as multi-part assembly
prompt.brief: 2.0 m radius tail-rotor blade: UD spar caps + plain-weave web + plain-weave skin + Ti root bushings; 1200 rpm centrifugal + flap/lag + BVID residual + 10^7 fatigue; CMH-17 B-basis with 0.80 hot/wet knockdown.

parts.name: spar_cap
parts.role: upper or lower UD IM7/8552 spar cap carrying blade axial/bending

parts.name: skin
parts.role: +/-45 plain-weave skin forming airfoil OML and torsion box

parts.name: root_bushing
parts.role: Ti-6Al-4V bushing through spar providing pitch-bearing attach

output_format.body_count: multi-body (spar caps + web + skin + root bushings)

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Xt B-basis RTD per CMH-17 IM7/8552 UD
0.85 x Xt B-basis damage-tolerance residual strength
CMH-17 S-N ~0.4 x Xt at 10^7 cycles for UD IM7/8552 at R=0.1
Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

interfaces.load_path: blade shear transferred from caps into web; drives Tsai-Wu index at interface plies
torsion box closure; OML aero load into spar
centrifugal tension reaction from blade into hub via pitch bearing

fem_check_policy.rationale: Spar-cap stresses (R1, R3) are composite-section properties spanning caps + webs + skin; Tsai-Wu (R4) is a ply-level check across all families; BVID residual (R5) spans damaged cap-web-skin interface; root-bushing transfer to spar (R1 at root) is an A-D interface. No family has meaningful isolated pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part_is_inherent: CMH-17 composite design is layup- and interface-driven; a rotor blade is by construction a built-up assembly of caps, web, skin, and bushings.

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Rotor dynamics (flap/lag/pitch, ground resonance) out of scope.
exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Hot/wet knockdown applied as scalar; no transient thermal.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Rotor caps tension-dominated under centrifugal; not driving.

eval_coverage: CMH-17 B-basis allowables on assembled blade
CMH-17 S-N 10^7 cycles fatigue at assembly level

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
